summary: Matched the SysDolphin baselib camera-object animation removal routine by replacing the assembly implementation of func_80367874 with C as HSD_CObjRemoveAnim, adding a small inline helper HSD_CObjRemoveAnimByFlags, wiring cobj.o into the epilogue build list, and promoting related WObj accessor symbols from anonymous func_8036862C/func_8036866C names to HSD_CObjGetInterestWObj and HSD_CObjGetEyePositionWObj. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice, so intent beyond the diff is inferred from the title and code changes.
what_changed: Removed the assembly body for func_80367874 from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.s and implemented the matched routine in src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c as HSD_CObjRemoveAnim.

Added inline HSD_CObjRemoveAnimByFlags(HSD_CObj* cobj, u32 flags), which null-checks cobj, removes cobj->aobj via HSD_AObjRemove, clears cobj->aobj to NULL, then removes animation from the eye-position and interest WObj values returned by HSD_CObjGetEyePositionWObj and HSD_CObjGetInterestWObj.

HSD_CObjRemoveAnim now null-checks cobj and calls HSD_CObjRemoveAnimByFlags(cobj, 0x7FF). The flags parameter is present but unused in the shown implementation, apparently to match the original call shape or expected library API pattern.
Renamed assembly globals func_8036862C and func_8036866C to HSD_CObjGetInterestWObj and HSD_CObjGetEyePositionWObj respectively, and updated callsites in cobj.s to use the new names.
Updated asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s to call HSD_CObjRemoveAnim instead of func_80367874 at lbl_801879C4.
Added cobj.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk, enabling src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c to contribute matched code in the epilogue object list.
Updated src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.h to include aobj.h and wobj.h and declare HSD_CObjRemoveAnim plus the two HSD_CObj WObj accessor functions.
Updated include/functions.h comments to reflect the newly identified HSD_CObjRemoveAnim, HSD_CObjGetInterestWObj, and HSD_CObjGetEyePositionWObj names.
smart_moves: Kept the C implementation structurally close to the removed assembly: explicit null guard, HSD_AObjRemove on offset-backed cobj->aobj, store NULL back to cobj->aobj, then two WObj accessor calls followed by HSD_WObjRemoveAnim.
Used #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around the matched C code, indicating a localized compiler/codegen control tactic rather than globally affecting the file.

Introduced meaningful HSD_CObjGetEyePositionWObj and HSD_CObjGetInterestWObj names before using them in the new C implementation, reducing reliance on anonymous func_* symbols and making the camera-object animation logic readable.
Preserved assembly callsite documentation by changing branch labels/comments from func_* to HSD_* names while leaving instruction bytes and addresses intact.
Added the necessary baselib includes in cobj.h so HSD_AObj and HSD_WObj types/functions are available through proper headers instead of relying only on loose declarations.
decomp_lessons: When matching a small wrapper/removal routine, it can be useful to factor the original behavior into an inline ByFlags helper even if a parameter is unused, because the original library API may have separate public and flag-based internal entry points.

For HSD object cleanup routines, match both the removal call and the state mutation: HSD_CObjRemoveAnim removes the AObj and then explicitly clears cobj->aobj to NULL before touching associated WObjs.
Promoting nearby accessor functions to real names can be a prerequisite for cleanly matching a caller in C; here the CObj WObj accessors were named and declared alongside HSD_CObjRemoveAnim.

Build integration is part of matching: after moving code from asm to src, the corresponding object must be added to the epilogue object list or the decompiled function will not replace the assembly version.
Localized pragma use is an accepted matching tactic in this slice, especially #pragma peephole on around sensitive baselib code.
naming_conventions: Camera-object baselib symbols use the HSD_CObj* prefix; world-object animation removal uses HSD_WObjRemoveAnim; animation-object removal uses HSD_AObjRemove.
The helper follows the library-style suffix pattern HSD_CObjRemoveAnimByFlags, while the public wrapper is HSD_CObjRemoveAnim.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a complete assembly function only after adding the matched C equivalent in src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c.
Used #pragma peephole on around HSD_CObjRemoveAnimByFlags and HSD_CObjRemoveAnim, suggesting peephole optimization was needed for the desired PowerPC output.
Implemented HSD_CObjRemoveAnim as a wrapper that passes constant 0x7FF into an inline helper, likely shaping codegen to match the original routine sequence.
Kept null-check early returns in both the helper and wrapper, matching the removed assembly's branch-to-epilogue behavior for NULL cobj.
Updated symbolic branch targets in remaining assembly rather than leaving stale func_* names, improving cross-file disassembly readability without changing machine instructions.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that implement or use HSD_CObjRemoveAnimByFlags with meaningful flag handling, since this slice shows the flags parameter but does not use it.
Check subsequent cobj.c matching work for whether HSD_CObjGetEyePositionWObj and HSD_CObjGetInterestWObj were moved from assembly to C.
Search for other HSD_*RemoveAnim routines to compare the wrapper-plus-ByFlags matching pattern and null-clearing behavior.
Verify whether adding cobj.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED remained the final build strategy or was later reorganized as more cobj functions matched.
